mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuations by lowering discount rates and supporting risk appetite, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is falling over the prior 3 months, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS sits above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation is still elevated, the market tends to price tighter financial conditions and multiple compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month change remains positive
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances produce positive 5-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the sign of the effect disappears once FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals deteriorating household income expectations and weaker demand, which should pressure equities and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not negative in at least 55% of months after the specified UNRATE rise, or if the signal loses directionality in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in RSAFS indicates strengthening retail demand and revenue growth expectations, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum fails to coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the rolling Rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rise in INDPRO above its recent trend indicates improving production and margin support, which should raise S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through better earnings revisions.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by more than 0.5% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show positive 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the effect reverses in rolling 4-quarter samples.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in HOUST and GDPC1 identifies a broad demand upturn, and that joint improvement should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because cyclicals and earnings expectations strengthen together.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition on HOUST and GDPC1 does not outperform either series alone in out-of-sample Rank IC, or if positive 60-day returns occur less than 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: If PAYEMS contracts while UNRATE rises, labor-market deterioration should signal weaker consumption and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls over 2 months and UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor deterioration condition fails to yield negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of occurrences, or if PAYEMS and UNRATE effects are independently sufficient without interaction.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a rising FEDFUNDS rate paired with sticky CPIAUCSL inflation compresses equity multiples and should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined tightening-plus-inflation setup does not produce negative 60-day returns more often than positive ones, or if the effect vanishes after controlling for either proxy alone.
